heroSubtext: Advancing transformative scholarship and applied projects that center justice, equity, and liberation. My work challenges traditional criminological frameworks while building new pathways toward healing and community empowerment.

researchAreas.title: Social Identity Theory of Shared Narrative
researchAreas.description: Exploring how collective narratives shape individual and group identities within criminal justice contexts, with particular attention to marginalized communities.

researchAreas.title: Affective Architecture
researchAreas.description: Investigating the emotional and psychological dimensions of criminal justice spaces and how they impact both practitioners and those processed through the system.

researchAreas.title: Decolonizing Criminology
researchAreas.description: Challenging traditional criminological frameworks through Indigenous, Black, and other non-Western epistemologies to create more inclusive and just approaches.

researchAreas.title: LGBTQ+ Justice
researchAreas.description: Examining the unique experiences of LGBTQ+ individuals in criminal justice systems and developing affirming, trauma-informed approaches to justice.

researchAreas.title: Ecological Justice
researchAreas.description: Connecting environmental harm with social justice, exploring how ecological destruction disproportionately affects marginalized communities.

currentProjects.title: Narrative Identity and Reentry Success
currentProjects.description: A longitudinal study examining how formerly incarcerated individuals construct and reconstruct their identities during reentry, with focus on narrative coherence and community support.

currentProjects.title: Queer Criminology Pedagogy Project
currentProjects.description: Developing inclusive curriculum and teaching materials that center LGBTQ+ experiences in criminology education.

currentProjects.title: Environmental Racism and Criminal Justice
currentProjects.description: Investigating the intersection of environmental harm and criminal justice involvement in communities of color.

appliedProjects.title: Decolonizing Criminology Initiative
appliedProjects.description: A comprehensive initiative to transform criminology education and research by centering Indigenous, Black, and other non-Western epistemologies.
appliedProjects.impact: Reached 500+ students, trained 25 faculty members, established partnerships with 8 community organizations
outcomes: Developed 6 new courses incorporating decolonial perspectives
Published pedagogical toolkit adopted by 12 universities
Established community advisory board for curriculum oversight

appliedProjects.title: LGBTQ+ Justice Policy Reform
appliedProjects.description: Led policy reform efforts to improve treatment of LGBTQ+ individuals in NYC criminal justice system, including training programs for law enforcement.
appliedProjects.impact: Trained 1,200+ officers, reduced discriminatory incidents by 40%, established LGBTQ+ liaison program
outcomes: Implemented mandatory LGBTQ+ cultural competency training
Established safe housing protocols for transgender individuals

appliedProjects.title: Narrative Healing Circles Program
appliedProjects.description: Developed narrative-based healing circles for formerly incarcerated individuals and their families, focusing on identity reconstruction and community reintegration.
appliedProjects.impact: Served 200+ participants, 85% successful reintegration rate, expanded to 5 community centers

appliedProjects.title: Environmental Justice and Incarceration Study
appliedProjects.description: Community-based participatory research examining the relationship between environmental racism and criminal justice involvement.
appliedProjects.impact: Documented environmental-criminal justice connections in 15 neighborhoods, informed 3 policy proposals
